skill.name: flux-kontext
skill.description: Guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through image edits with Flux 1 Kontext Pro on RunComfy. It documents when to use the model, how to prepare a single source image and prompt, and how to invoke the RunComfy CLI for precise local edits.
skill.summary: Edit a single source image with Flux 1 Kontext Pro through the RunComfy CLI.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, and blocker patterns, but most are markdown examples, service URLs, or false positives from model and heading text. The remaining real concerns are legitimate but material: the skill instructs agents to run the RunComfy CLI, upload image URLs and prompts to a third-party model API, and write outputs to a local directory.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Invocation Required
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run the local RunComfy CLI, including command examples and installation guidance. This is not malicious by itself, but it expands execution risk because the agent may invoke a local binary with user-supplied prompt and image data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly tells the agent to invoke runcomfy commands. The risk is bounded because the prompt is described as JSON input rather than shell-expanded text.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Image Processing Endpoint
medium_findings.description: The skill sends prompts and source image references to RunComfy model infrastructure and downloads generated results. Users must treat source images, prompts, and generated outputs as data shared with an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is stated directly, including the model API endpoint and output download domains. This is expected for the skill but still affects privacy and data handling.

low_findings.title: Local Output and Token File Touchpoints
low_findings.description: The skill documents output directory writes and RunComfy token storage under the user configuration directory. The token storage note says the file uses owner-only permissions, so this is a disclosure and local hygiene concern rather than confirmed credential exposure.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem interactions are explicitly documented. I did not find evidence of arbitrary file reads, path traversal, or unsafe permission changes in the skill text.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm and path traversal detections do not correspond to dangerous behavior in context. The cited lines are frontmatter text, headings, model descriptions, URL placeholders, or documentation, not executable cryptography or path traversal logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is markdown documentation and example data. I did not find executable weak crypto, traversal logic, or malicious intent at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Execution Pattern in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on users or agents running npx, npm, runcomfy login, and runcomfy run commands. These commands are expected for this integration but should be reviewed before use in restricted environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Multiple command examples are present and intended to be executed. The surrounding documentation provides normal operational context rather than concealment.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Network Transfer Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill describes sending input to RunComfy over HTTPS and fetching generated output from RunComfy domains. This is core functionality and should be disclosed to users handling sensitive images.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and domain whitelist are explicitly documented. The behavior is expected, but the data transfer is real.

content.user_title: Edit Images with Flux Kontext Pro
content.value_statement: Precise image edits are hard to prompt consistently across models. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use Flux Kontext Pro through RunComfy with focused prompts and clear routing rules.
actual_capabilities: Selects Flux Kontext Pro for precise edits to one source image.
Explains the RunComfy CLI command for the model endpoint.
Documents required inputs for prompt, image URL, aspect ratio, and seed.
Provides prompt patterns that preserve identity, pose, lighting, and brand details.
Routes multi-image, text-heavy, or text-to-image requests to more suitable models.
Summarizes RunComfy exit codes and troubleshooting references.
limitations: Requires a RunComfy account, CLI installation, and valid authentication.
Works with one source image, not multi-image edit batches.
Requires publicly fetchable HTTPS image URLs for the documented workflow.
Sends prompts and image references to RunComfy model infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Retouch a Product Image
use_cases.description: Change label text, colors, or small product details while preserving lighting and composition.

use_cases.title: Edit a Portrait Detail
use_cases.description: Add or change one visual element while keeping the person, pose, and clothing stable.

use_cases.title: Prototype Visual Variants
use_cases.description: Use seeds and concise prompts to compare controlled variations from one source image.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Local Edit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one clear change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Flux Kontext Pro to edit this image. Keep the main subject unchanged. Add [specific object or detail] in [specific location].

prompt_templates.title: Preserve Identity Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Flux Kontext Pro. Keep the face, pose, clothing, camera angle, and lighting unchanged. Change only [one target detail] to [new detail].

prompt_templates.title: Brand Asset Revision
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Flux Kontext Pro. Keep the product shape, background, shadows, and label layout unchanged. Replace only [current brand element] with [new brand element].

prompt_templates.title: Reproducible Variant Series
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced comparison workflow with a seed.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Flux Kontext Pro with seed [number]. Create one controlled variation that changes [single detail]. Preserve all other visible attributes from the source image.

output_examples.input: A user asks to add an orange umbrella to a portrait while preserving the subject.
output_examples.output: The assistant chooses Flux Kontext Pro, writes a preservation-led prompt, and runs the RunComfy edit workflow for one image.

output_examples.input: A designer asks to replace product label text without changing the bottle or lighting.
output_examples.output: The assistant creates a narrow brand-edit prompt that changes only the label text and keeps composition stable.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a batch edit across many reference images.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains that Flux Kontext Pro is single-image focused and routes the task to a better multi-image workflow.

best_practices: Use one source image and one clear edit instruction per run.
Start prompts with the details that must remain unchanged.
Use a seed when comparing small visual variations.
anti_patterns: Do not combine several unrelated edits in one prompt.
Do not use this skill for multi-image batch editing.
Do not upload sensitive images unless RunComfy processing is acceptable.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an AI assistant edit one image with Flux Kontext Pro through the RunComfy CLI.

faq.question: Does it generate images from scratch?
faq.answer: No. It is documented for image-to-image editing from a single source image.

faq.question: What tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What input does it need?
faq.answer: It needs a prompt and a publicly fetchable HTTPS image URL. Aspect ratio and seed are optional.

faq.question: Where is my image processed?
faq.answer: The documented workflow sends the prompt and image reference to RunComfy model infrastructure.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning for external CLI execution, network transfer, and local output files.
